site.name: National CAD Standard
site.tagline: The single nationally recognized standard for the organization and presentation of construction documents.
site.address: National Institute of Building Sciences, Washington, DC

hero.kicker: United States National CAD Standard® · Version 7
hero.title: One standard. Every drawing set.
hero.subtitle: The NCS unifies the AIA CAD Layer Guidelines, the CSI Uniform Drawing System, and the NIBS BIM Implementation & Plotting Guidelines into one coordinated standard — now reimagined as an immersive, fully manageable web experience.

intro.title: Three standards, coordinated into one
intro.body: Since 1999 the U.S. National CAD Standard has given architects, engineers and agencies a common language for construction documents — consistent layers, sheets, symbols, notations and plotting across every discipline and firm.

features.title: AIA CAD Layer Guidelines
features.body: A complete, coordinated layer naming system so every discipline reads the same model the same way.

features.title: CSI Uniform Drawing System
features.body: Eight modules governing set organization, sheets, schedules, drafting conventions, terms, symbols, notations and code conventions.

features.title: NIBS BIM & Plotting Guidelines
features.body: BIM implementation guidance plus standardized plotting — line weights, screening and pen tables — for predictable output.

about.title: About the National CAD Standard
about.lead: The United States National CAD Standard® (NCS) is the single, nationally recognized standard for organizing and presenting building design data.
about.body: The NCS is a consensus standard published and maintained by the National Institute of Building Sciences (NIBS). It combines work from three founding organizations — the American Institute of Architects (AIA), the Construction Specifications Institute (CSI), and NIBS — so that drawing sets are coordinated and legible no matter who produced them. Version 7 is delivered as a licensed online document with 24/7 access from the office, plus downloadable Excel, DWG, LIN and PAT data files.

principles.title: Consensus-built
principles.body: Developed and balloted by AIA, CSI and NIBS with industry-wide participation.

principles.title: Coordinated
principles.body: Layers, sheets, symbols and notations align across every discipline and firm.

principles.title: Maintained
principles.body: Published and kept current by NIBS, version after version, since 1999.

timeline.event: NCS Version 1 published — AIA, CSI and NIBS unify their standards.
Version 3 / 3.1 expands the Uniform Drawing System modules.
Version 7 — current edition, delivered as a licensed online document.

products.title: Foreword & Administration
products.summary: How the standard is structured, adopted and applied on a project.
products.description: The Foreword and Administration sections explain the purpose of the NCS, how to reference and adopt it on a project, and how the component standards fit together.

products.title: AIA CAD Layer Guidelines
products.summary: The coordinated layer naming system used across all disciplines.
products.description: A complete, hierarchical CAD layer naming convention (discipline designators, major/minor groups, status) with downloadable Excel reference tables.

products.title: Uniform Drawing System (8 Modules)
products.summary: Set organization, sheets, schedules, drafting conventions, terms, symbols, notations and code conventions.
products.description: The eight UDS modules: (1) Drawing Set Organization, (2) Sheet Organization, (3) Schedules, (4) Drafting Conventions, (5) Terms & Abbreviations, (6) Symbols, (7) Notations, (8) Code Conventions. Includes downloadable DWG/LIN/PAT symbol files.

products.title: BIM Implementation
products.summary: Guidance for applying the standard within BIM workflows.
products.description: How the NCS conventions map onto Building Information Modeling — object naming, coordination and deliverables.

products.title: Plotting Guidelines
products.summary: Standardized line weights, screening and pen tables for predictable output.
products.description: Plotting Guidelines define line weights, screening percentages and pen-table assignments so printed and PDF output is consistent across firms. Includes Excel tables.

products.title: Appendixes & Data Files
products.summary: Downloadable Excel, DWG, LIN and PAT files that accompany the online document.
products.description: Excel files (AIA Layer Guidelines, UDS Terms & Abbreviations, Schedules, Regulatory Information, Plotting tables) and DWG/LIN/PAT files (UDS Symbols), downloadable from the licensed online document.

pricing.title: Order the NCS
pricing.subtitle: Version 7 is a licensed online document with 24/7 access. Choose the license that matches your team. (Prices shown are illustrative for this demo — actual pricing is set at checkout.)

plans.name: Single License
plans.blurb: One to two employees within a single office at a single location.

plans.name: Site License
plans.blurb: Bulk access for a single office — up to ten employees.

plans.name: Enterprise License
plans.blurb: Company-wide access across multiple offices under a single domain.

resources.title: Downloads & FAQ
resources.subtitle: Data files that ship with the online document, plus answers to the questions licensees ask most.

items.title: AIA CAD Layer Guidelines (Excel)
items.body: The full layer list as a downloadable Excel workbook for reference and customization.

items.title: UDS Symbols (DWG / LIN / PAT)
items.body: CAD-ready symbol, linetype and hatch-pattern files from the Uniform Drawing System.

items.title: Plotting Tables (Excel)
items.body: Line-weight, screening and pen-table assignments in spreadsheet form.

items.title: View a CAD drawing in 3D
items.body: See how a flat drawing becomes an explorable, exploded 3D model — the new way to present the standard.

items.title: What is included in V7?
items.body: Foreword, Administration, AIA CAD Layer Guidelines, the 8-module Uniform Drawing System, BIM Implementation, Plotting Guidelines and Appendixes.

items.title: How is access delivered?
items.body: As a licensed online document with 24/7 access, plus downloadable Excel/DWG/LIN/PAT data files.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Questions about licensing, content, or a custom rollout? Reach the NCS team.

office.name: National Institute of Building Sciences
office.address: Washington, DC (publisher of the U.S. National CAD Standard®)

whatsnew.title: What's New in Version 7
whatsnew.intro: Version 7 is the current edition of the United States National CAD Standard®, delivered as a licensed online document.

sections.body: V7 moved from a static document to a licensed online format — licensees access the standard any time from the office, with downloadable data files alongside the text.
The edition refreshes the AIA CAD Layer Guidelines, the CSI Uniform Drawing System modules, and the NIBS BIM Implementation and Plotting Guidelines, keeping conventions aligned with current practice.

faqs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqs.intro: The questions licensees ask most about content, access and licensing.

sections.body: Foreword, Administration, AIA CAD Layer Guidelines, the eight-module Uniform Drawing System, BIM Implementation, Plotting Guidelines and Appendixes — plus downloadable Excel, DWG, LIN and PAT data files.
As a licensed online document with 24/7 access. After signing in, licensed users view the full text and download the data files. The public site shows program information only.

sections.h: What are the eight UDS modules?

sections.body: Single (1–2 users, one office), Site (3–10 users, one office), or Enterprise (11+ users, multiple offices under one domain). See the Order page.
It is a consensus standard published and maintained by the National Institute of Building Sciences (NIBS), combining work from AIA, CSI and NIBS.

news.title: News
news.intro: Announcements about the United States National CAD Standard.

sections.body: The current edition is available to license as an online document with 24/7 access and downloadable data files.
The NCS continues to be referenced by firms and agencies as the common basis for organizing and presenting construction documents.

press.title: Press Releases
press.intro: Official releases from the National Institute of Building Sciences regarding the NCS.

sections.body: Press releases announce new versions, partnerships and milestones for the United States National CAD Standard. (Demo placeholder content — real releases drop in via the CMS.)

copyright.title: Copyright & Trademark
copyright.intro: Usage and ownership of the United States National CAD Standard®.

sections.body: The United States National CAD Standard® is a registered trademark of the National Institute of Building Sciences. The NCS combines copyrighted material from AIA, CSI and NIBS.
Access is granted under Single, Site or Enterprise license terms. Printed-copy limits vary by license. This site is a demonstration recreation for evaluation and is not the official NCS.

footer.blurb: The United States National CAD Standard® unifies the AIA CAD Layer Guidelines, the CSI Uniform Drawing System, and the NIBS BIM & Plotting Guidelines into one coordinated standard.
footer.legal: © 2026 National Institute of Building Sciences. The United States National CAD Standard® is a registered trademark of NIBS. This is a demonstration recreation for evaluation.
